commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: feet: By the "fiery feet" he means the Church of the last time, which is to be searched and proved by severe afflictions. For orichalcum is brass, which by much fire and various ingredients, is brought to the colour of gold. Another translation, which renders it, "like orichalcum of Lebanon," signifies that in Judæa, of which Lebanon is a mountain, the Church will be persecuted, and especially at the last. The temple also frequently received the name of Lebanon, as there is said to it, "Open, O Lebanon, thy gates, and let the fire devour thy cedars."

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: voice: The voice of confession, and preaching, and praise does not resound in Judæa alone, but among many peoples. <a

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
His feet like unto fine brass, to signify the purity and steadfastness of his steps and actions.
His voice as the sound of many waters, the sound of his preaching by himself, and by his apostles, has been heard throughout all nations of the world. (Witham)

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: On this account also does John declare in the Apocalypse, "And His voice as the sound of many waters."

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: The many waters are understood to be many peoples, or the gift of baptism that He sent forth by the apostles, saying: Go, teach all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost. Matthew 28:19

commentaries.author: Victorinus of Pettau
commentaries.content: And his feet like unto fine bronze, as if they burned in a furnace; and his voice as the sound of many waters. Victorinus of Pettau He calls the apostles His feet, who, being wrought by suffering, preached His word in the whole world; for He rightly named those by whose means the preaching went forth, feet. Whence also the prophet anticipated this, and said: We will worship in the place where His feet have stood. Because where they first of all stood and confirmed the Church, that is, in Judea, all the saints shall assemble together, and will worship their Lord.
